4+.description: Content suitable for ages 4 and up. Designed for young children with no objectionable material. Maps to Apple's age-based rating used in Screen Time and Family Sharing restrictions.

9+.description: Content suitable for ages 9 and up. May include mild or infrequent cartoon, fantasy, or realistic violence, and infrequent or mild mature, suggestive, or horror-themed content.

12+.description: Content suitable for ages 12 and up. May include infrequent mild language, frequent or intense cartoon, fantasy, or realistic violence, and mild or infrequent mature or suggestive themes.

17+.description: No content restrictions. All content visible including mature and adult material. Unrestricted access equivalent to the default adult experience on Apple TV+.

tv_shows.notes: Web parental controls at tv.apple.com allow TV show and movie ratings to be set independently. When a maximum rating is selected, all content above that rating is hidden.

movies.notes: Movie ratings are configured separately from TV show ratings on the web. The 'Don't Allow' option blocks all movies regardless of rating.

content_descriptors.notes: Apple TV+ displays content descriptors (e.g., 'Violence, Language') on title detail pages. These align with Apple's App Store age-based rating system (4+, 9+, 12+, 17+). Filtering is age-tier-based only -- individual descriptor filtering is not supported. Apple uses the same 4-tier age rating system across the App Store, Apple TV+, and Screen Time.

MPAA.notes: Apple TV+ shows MPA (formerly MPAA) ratings for films. NC-17 content is rare on the platform. NR (Not Rated) applies to some Apple Original documentaries.

TV_Parental_Guidelines.notes: Full TV rating coverage for series. Apple TV+ original series use standard TV Parental Guidelines ratings.

ESRB.notes: Apple TV+ does not host games. Apple Arcade is a separate service with its own age ratings managed via the App Store.

PEGI.notes: Not applicable to Apple TV+ streaming content. PEGI ratings apply to Apple Arcade games separately.

Common_Sense_Media.notes: Apple TV app displays Common Sense Media age recommendations on some titles as supplementary guidance, but these are not used for content filtering or restriction enforcement.

Apple_Age_Rating.notes: Apple's proprietary age-based rating system is the primary rating mechanism for Screen Time and Family Sharing. Used across Apple TV+, App Store, and Screen Time. Content restrictions in Family Sharing and Screen Time map directly to these tiers. The web player at tv.apple.com uses standard MPAA/TV Parental Guidelines ratings instead.

platform_specific_notes.dual_rating_systems: Apple uses TWO rating systems for content restrictions: (1) Apple age tiers (4+, 9+, 12+, 17+) used by Screen Time and Family Sharing on devices, and (2) standard MPAA + TV Parental Guidelines ratings used by the web player at tv.apple.com. These do not sync with each other.
platform_specific_notes.separate_tv_movie_ratings: The web player at tv.apple.com allows TV show and movie rating restrictions to be set INDEPENDENTLY. This is different from Netflix which uses unified maturity tiers. Parents can set TV-PG for shows but PG-13 for movies, for example.
platform_specific_notes.web_vs_device_independence: Rating restrictions set on tv.apple.com (web) are INDEPENDENT from device-level restrictions (Screen Time / tvOS Restrictions). They do not sync.
platform_specific_notes.international_ratings: Apple TV+ supports country-specific rating systems internationally. This mapping covers US ratings only. Apple partners with local content rating bodies in each country.
